focus_points.label: What “good” feels like right now.
focus_points.reason: What expectations and success bar feel most uncertain for him after three months in. Could be shifting requirements, unclear ownership, or just the normal dip—worth clarifying what specifically feels fuzzy.

focus_points.label: How safe it feels to ask for help.
focus_points.reason: Whether he feels connected to support and safe raising confusion or frustration. One possibility is he’s trying to prove himself by absorbing uncertainty rather than asking early.

focus_points.label: Where the team setup makes it harder.
focus_points.reason: If the role feels harder than it needs to be, this is the fastest way to find the friction points you can remove—process, review patterns, access to context, or how help is requested and received.

focus_points.label: What he’s saying yes to versus no to.
focus_points.reason: What’s pulling his attention between proving himself and the actual highest-leverage work. This helps separate “shifted requirements” from a broader mismatch in priorities or sequencing.
